items.title: Enhance Kubernetes API Read Resilience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a retry mechanism for read-only `kubectl get` calls in the production deploy script. By implementing this change, we can handle transient API timeouts more effectively, ensuring smoother deployments without altering the rollout order or traffic switching behavior. The modifications aim to improve the reliability of static release readiness and endpoint checks.

items.title: Enhance Deployment Resilience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the deployment process by ensuring that the static release pods are fully running and ready before proceeding. It also enhances the resilience of endpoint convergence against transient pod deletions, addressing issues from previous deployments. Key modifications were made to the deployment script to implement these changes effectively.

items.title: Enhance Static Frontend Rollout Timing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update extends the static frontend rollout wait time from 10 minutes to 20 minutes, allowing for smoother deployments. Additionally, it introduces diagnostic outputs for deployment and pod statuses if the rollout fails, improving troubleshooting capabilities. These changes align CI processes with observed rollout latencies, enhancing overall deployment reliability.

items.title: Enhance Nexior Scenario UI Consistency in Index 🎨
items.summary: This update proposes a comprehensive audit and unification plan for the Nexior scenario UI, focusing on 20 generated workflows. Key changes include a structured approach to standardizing UI components and interactions across different media types, while ensuring no immediate modifications to the product UI. The plan aims to improve user experience by addressing inconsistencies in parameter controls and media previews.

items.title: Enhance SSR Deployment Stability in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the SSR Deployment configuration by setting the `progressDeadlineSeconds` to 1500 seconds, ensuring Kubernetes does not prematurely fail the rollout. Additionally, diagnostics for Deployment and pod statuses are now printed if the SSR rollout encounters issues, improving troubleshooting capabilities.

items.title: Give SSR rollouts extra breathing room in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: Increased the SSR rollout timeout from 10 minutes to 20 minutes to allow deployments more time to complete. Added a 120-second termination grace period for SSR pods so in-flight renders can finish during rollouts. Updated deployment validation scripts to confirm the rollout configuration changes cleanly.

items.title: Enhance Task Management with Router Guards in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves task management by preserving independent task persistence for each provider worker while suppressing client task writes from router-managed attempts. Key changes include the propagation of the verified `routerManaged` state and the introduction of AST contracts for better write management. The update ensures that all affected clients handle task writes correctly, maintaining the integrity of independent and managed executions.

items.title: Activate Xiaohongshu Browser Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update activates the Xiaohongshu browser connector as part of the unified browser connection rollout. It includes the deletion of legacy browser session rows with empty identifiers while preserving valid connections. The connector is now set to visible and installable, ensuring a smoother integration for users.

items.title: Streamline Connector Auth Models for Unified Connections in Index 🚀
items.summary: This PR outlines a comprehensive plan to align browser connectors with a unified Connection model, ensuring consistency across various auth modes. Key changes include the removal of unnecessary duplication in the Connector installation process and the introduction of a clear structure for creating user Connections. The plan has been validated against the existing model, confirming its integrity and readiness for review.

items.title: Streamline Airwallex Secrets for Deployment in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the requirement for the Airwallex secrets during deployment when the bank transfer feature is disabled. The reconciliation process now skips gracefully if the provider token is not configured, enhancing deployment flexibility. Additionally, documentation has been updated to clarify the need for provisioning secrets before enabling the server flag.

items.title: Enhance Payment Options with Airwallex Bank Transfer in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Bank Transfer UI for Airwallex-backed USD orders, requiring explicit tester opt-in and server-side configuration. The feature is gated for orders priced at $5,000 or more, ensuring only eligible transactions can utilize this payment method. Additionally, existing bank instructions will remain visible post-payment creation, enhancing user experience.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Connector Integration in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update binds the Xiaohongshu browser execution to its specific Connector identifier, ensuring accurate resolution and preventing alias conflicts. Key changes include the addition of a contract assertion for the canonical provider and successful validation of the Xiaohongshu browser contract. This enhancement improves the reliability of workflows involving Xiaohongshu.

items.title: Enhance Browser Connector Resolution in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the aichat2 browser execution strictly resolves to an exact canonical Connector Connection. Key changes include the removal of alias fallbacks and the introduction of explicit requirements for Connector identifiers, enhancing security and clarity in browser execution. Additionally, negative tests have been added to validate the new selection criteria.

items.title: Enhance Browser Connector Device Selection in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated BrowserDevice picker for the AuthFrontend, streamlining the installation flow for browser connectors. Key features include displaying allowed origins, device compatibility, and auto-selecting compatible devices. Additionally, the dialog is now responsive and supports localization across 17 languages.

items.title: Unified Browser Connectors Implementation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust foundation for browser-backed Connectors within the AuthBackend. Key changes include the acceptance of `browser_device_id` in installation requests, enhanced validation of device ownership, and the creation of a unique `Connection` for each browser session. The update also allows a single BrowserDevice to support multiple Connectors, streamlining the connection process and improving overall system efficiency.

items.title: Enhance Wisdom Lifecycle Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the Wisdom runtime lifecycle into the PlatformFrontend, utilizing the `GET /api/status` endpoint as the sole source of lifecycle information. Key states such as 'starting', 'waiting_for_login', 'preparing', 'ready', and 'error' are now supported, improving user feedback during the login process. Additionally, the QR code handling has been refined to ensure accurate results and prevent old data from interfering with new requests.

items.title: Update readiness probe to use health endpoint in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Wisdom AKS appliance readiness probe to transition from the `/api/ping` endpoint to the more reliable `/api/health`. This change ensures that the business lifecycle state does not affect ingress accessibility. The affected manifest tests have been validated, with a successful outcome on 20 out of 21 tests.

items.title: Unified Health Checks for Wisdom API in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the health check endpoints for the Wisdom API, replacing `/api/ping` with a unified `/api/health` endpoint. Key documentation and deployment templates have been updated accordingly to reflect this change, ensuring a smoother operational experience. The modifications enhance the reliability of health checks during service startup.

items.title: Streamline Order Updates in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the order editing process by directing admin edits to the newly named `update-details` endpoint, while removing the outdated `update-price` call from the frontend. These changes ensure a more efficient and accurate handling of order details, improving overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Asset Safety for Frontend Releases in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues from overlapping rollouts that caused asset retrieval failures in production. Key changes include serialized production workflows, improved asset retention strategies, and enhanced deployment scripts to ensure stability during updates. The modifications aim to prevent future incidents and ensure a smoother user experience.

items.title: Enhance V5.5 Replace Section Functionality in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the `replace_section` requests for the `chirp-v5-5` model by ensuring explicit bounded infill context is sent. It validates the requested range against the source clip duration, preserving existing behaviors and adding a focused regression test for V5.5. The changes enhance the reliability of the model while maintaining compatibility with previous versions.

items.title: Streamline Airwallex Transfers with Automation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated Airwallex bank transfers for verified enterprise customers, ensuring a seamless payment experience. Key features include direct webhook handling, enhanced security with HMAC verification, and a fallback reconciliation process. The implementation also includes new models and management commands to support these functionalities.

items.title: Update grok CLI docs to align with OpenAPI spec in Clis 🚀
items.summary: The grok CLI documentation has been updated to reflect changes in the OpenAPI specification. Key updates include the addition of `grok-4.5` to the chat model list and corrections to video model names, ensuring they match the current spec. No code changes were necessary as the CLI already handles the updated duration default seamlessly.

items.title: Enhance Order Details Update Route in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `POST /api/v1/orders/{id}/update-details/` admin action while retaining the `update-price` alias for backward compatibility. It ensures that only validated fields are persisted to prevent overwriting concurrent order state changes and includes a regression test for route registration. The changes enhance the overall functionality and reliability of order management.

items.title: Exciting New Global Account APIs for Airwallex in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the standard Airwallex provider adapter with new endpoints for managing Global Accounts. Key features include support for opaque local payment IDs and the ability to independently query Airwallex Deposit data, all while keeping PayBackend stateless. The implementation is validated with a full pytest suite, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Update Grok Video Default Duration to 6 Seconds in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update changes the default video duration for Grok video generation from 8 seconds to a stable 6 seconds. The adjustment ensures consistency across initial state, reset behavior, selector fallback, and request submission. Additionally, a patch release note has been added for user clarity.

items.title: Align Grok video defaults and billing duration in Backend 🔧
items.summary: Updated Grok video API defaults and examples to use a 6-second duration while keeping existing duration ranges supported. Adjusted billing fallbacks, OpenAPI contracts, documentation examples, and display pricing tests to match the new default. Added coverage for omitted-duration cost evaluation and refreshed user-facing error wording.

items.title: Set Grok Video Duration to Six Seconds in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update establishes a shared default video duration of six seconds for Grok video generation. The change is reflected in both request metadata and the outgoing generation payload, ensuring consistency across the platform. Additionally, a new test has been added to verify the default duration, enhancing the reliability of the worker test suite.

items.title: Make Admin Order Names Editable in Frontend Web ✏️
items.summary: This update introduces a 255-character input field for editing admin order names, replacing the static description. The order name and price can now be saved together using the updated functionality. Additionally, the operator payload type has been narrowed to focus on these two editable fields, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Admin Order Editing Capabilities in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update allows superusers to edit both the description and price of orders simultaneously, with validation for a maximum description length of 255 characters and non-negative prices. New regression tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended, enhancing the overall order management experience for admins.

items.title: Enhance Order Discount Clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces human-readable discount percentages while maintaining the backend's 0–1 ratio. Customers will now see their actual payment percentage in the dialog and table, with discounts over 30% highlighted in red for better visibility. Additionally, all locale validation ranges have been updated to reflect the new 0–100 percentage format.

items.title: Refresh SSH gateway documentation status and rollout checklist in Wisdom 📝
items.summary: Updated the SSH gateway design document to reflect merged design status and current implementation progress. The documentation now tracks open implementation slices, validation state, remaining deployment gates, and an evidence-based rollout checklist while keeping deployment disabled until readiness requirements are met.

items.title: Revamp Email Marketing UI for Consistency in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update aligns the email marketing workbench with the existing Element Plus design system, ensuring consistency across light and dark modes. Key changes include standardizing card styles, updating color variables, and normalizing spacing within the email marketing module. The rendered email canvas remains unchanged, preserving its original styling while enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Update aegis-web-sdk to latest version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the aegis-web-sdk from version 1.41.13 to 1.41.14. Key changes include updates in the package-lock and package.json files, ensuring compatibility with the latest features and fixes. This enhancement aims to improve the overall performance and stability of the frontend application.

items.title: Streamline video commands by removing deprecated element_list in Clis 🎥
items.summary: This update removes the `element_list` field from the video commands in response to changes in the upstream API. The `--element-id` option and related helper functions have been eliminated from the `generate` and `image-to-video` commands, along with corresponding tests. All other fields remain aligned with the current OpenAPI specification.

items.title: Update readiness probe to API health check in Platform Deployment 🚀
items.summary: The readiness probe for the legacy `acedatacloud-wisdom` Helm chart has been updated from the business endpoint `/api/status` to the health endpoint `/api/health`. This change ensures that instances remain accessible for user interactions during login and data preparation phases. The modifications were validated through template path checks and `git diff` assessments.

items.title: Enhance Runtime Lifecycle Management in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new public lifecycle for the API, improving the handling of system states during data preparation. Key changes include the isolation of WCDB processes to reduce CPU load, a refined health check endpoint, and enhanced error handling for service readiness. These improvements aim to boost system stability and responsiveness, particularly during high-load scenarios.

items.title: Update vue-tsc to 3.3.7 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the vue-tsc package from version 3.3.5 to 3.3.7. Key improvements include fixes for single expression event handlers and filtering const globals from template completions, enhancing overall performance and reliability in TypeScript support.

items.title: Designing a Shared SSH Gateway for Wisdom in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design for a shared SSH gateway for Wisdom tenants, allowing secure access without the need for individual public IPs. Key features include a centralized SSH endpoint, high availability with sshpiper, and enhanced security measures like host key pinning. The documentation details the architecture, security boundaries, and deployment strategies to ensure a robust implementation.

items.title: Refine File Transfer Assistant Search Logic in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the search functionality for the File Transfer Assistant by ensuring it only selects results from the appropriate '功能' section. Key changes include the introduction of a new constant for section names and adjustments to the search result filtering logic. The fix prevents incorrect navigation to the web search when similar names appear in different contexts.
